codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature record, final handoff, transcript skeleton for worker session `087646d1-1f4a-4923-bd41-aa6a64ddbbde`, and commit `094878f66cdc67498474481303f07929c01ca5d0`. The change set preserves `AccountingFacade` as the external boundary while removing the targeted internal re-entry seams in the touched accounting corridors: manual journal delegation now routes through `JournalEntryService`, dealer receipt and supplier payment posting services now create journals directly through `JournalEntryService`, settlement auto-flows now call the focused `DealerReceiptService` and `SupplierPaymentService`, and period/reconciliation workflows now depend on `JournalEntryService` instead of looping back through `AccountingFacade`. The committed tests were updated to reflect the new seam ownership, and I did not find a high-confidence correctness regression in the touched corridors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The accounting-hardcut worker skill still treats `commands.gate-fast` as the default final-proof step, but this feature’s verification contract was intentionally narrower (`test-characterization`, `compile`, `targeted-accounting-proof`). That mismatch forced the worker to mark a justified deviation even though the packet followed its explicit feature-level contract.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature contract at `/Users/anas/.factory/missions/b3885fb3-3080-480f-9f59-1eba48c5f565/features.json:901-905` requires only `commands.test-characterization`, `commands.compile`, and `commands.targeted-accounting-proof`; skill guidance at `/Users/anas/Documents/Factory/bigbrightpaints-erp_worktrees/accounting-centralization-20260420/.factory/skills/accounting-hardcut-worker/SKILL.md:88` says to run `commands.gate-fast` for final PR-readiness unless explicitly scoped otherwise; handoff skill feedback at `/Users/anas/.factory/missions/b3885fb3-3080-480f-9f59-1eba48c5f565/handoffs/2026-04-21T05-49-57-924Z__accounting-facade-layer-seams-hardcut__087646d1-1f4a-4923-bd41-aa6a64ddbbde.json:73-79` records that exact deviation and recommends updating the skill wording.

summary: PASS: commit `094878f66cdc67498474481303f07929c01ca5d0` preserves `AccountingFacade` as the external accounting boundary while removing the targeted internal facade re-entry seams in settlement, receipt/payment posting, period-close/reopen, and reconciliation flows. I did not find any blocking or non-blocking code review issues in the committed feature diff; the only follow-up is a shared-state skill mismatch around when feature-level verification contracts may override the generic `gate-fast` recommendation.
